title: Low-Cost Handling and Distribution Devices for Experimental Work with Chironomids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3hirr","text":"This protocol describes the construction and use of custom-built tools for handling chironomid larvae and adults under laboratory conditions. The approaches include (1) a rotary dispensing device designed to achieve uniform distribution of larvae into multiple test tubes, (2) a modified pipette system for gentle aspiration and manual counting of larvae, and (3) a low-suction aspirator device for the safe collection of adult chironomids using a USB-powered vacuum and a custom collection chamber. These tools were developed to ensure precise counting, minimize handling stress, and improve reproducibility during experimental setup. Detailed material lists and step-by-step assembly instructions are provided to facilitate replication.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"gp4a","text":"Chironomid larval distribution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2o4de","text":"Maintain a steady rotation speed to avoid uneven allocation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4dqlg","text":"Pour slowly to prevent overflow or splashing.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5kvrj","text":"Ensure tube positions remain evenly spaced throughout rotation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66l7e","text":"The number of tubes can be adjusted depending on experimental design.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5n2me","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e37sc","text":"Chironomid larval counting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e8e09","text":"Avoid aspirating too many larvae at once to prevent overcrowding or stress.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"12p8o","text":"Maintain gentle pipetting to minimize larval injury.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ikcj","text":"Make sure glass tip is intact, larvae can stick to and be injured by sharp edges. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"279mc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3lsre","text":"Chironomid adult sampling","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1bpio","text":"Ensure all connections are airtight to maintain consistent suction.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4fe3","text":"Use low suction to prevent injury to specimens.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nvsm","text":"Regularly check the mesh barrier to prevent clogging.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the rotary basket Evenly distribute glass test tubes along the outer edge inside the salad spinner basket. Ensure all tubes are upright and stable during rotation.

Prepare the collection chamber Cut two holes into the lid of a 50 ml Falcon tube, sized to tightly fit the flexible tubing. Insert one piece of tubing into each hole. Seal both insertion points with hot glue to ensure an airtight fit.

Install the protective mesh barrier Inside the Falcon tube, cover the inner opening of the tubing that will connect to the suction device with a piece of fine mesh. Secure the mesh with tape to prevent insects from being drawn into the suction device.

Connect the suction system Attach the external end of one tube to the keyboard vacuum. If necessary, use a shortened 10 ml plastic pipette tip as an adapter to ensure a tight fit between tubing and suction device.
Collect adult chironomids Switch on the suction device. Use the free tube end to gently aspirate adult chironomids. Insects will be drawn into and retained inside the Falcon tube.
Retrieve collected specimens Turn off the suction device before opening the tube. Carefully remove the lid and transfer collected insects as required.
Prepare the modified pipette Attach a small glass pipette to the plastic tip of a standard piston pipette using tape. Ensure the glass tip is securely fastened and sterile if necessary.

Set up the dispensing system Mount a glass Pasteur pipette tip vertically in a laboratory stand. Attach a small funnel to the top of the pipette to facilitate controlled pouring. Position the pipette above the rotating basket so that the outflow is aligned with the tube openings.

Dispense larvae Gently swirl or agitate the larval medium to ensure larvae are evenly suspended. Begin rotating the salad spinner at a steady, moderate speed. Pour the larval medium into the funnel in a thin, continuous stream. Maintain constant rotation to ensure even distribution across all tubes.
Verify distribution After dispensing, stop rotation. Inspect tubes visually to confirm uniform larval distribution.
Count larvae Under a stereomicroscope, slowly aspirate larvae into the pipette while gently releasing the piston, counting each larva as it enters.
Transfer larvae Carefully dispense the counted larvae into the designated vessel. Record the number transferred. Repeat the process until the desired number of larvae is collected.
creator: Nina Röder
